title: corchorus obclavatus
intro:
Corchorus olivaceus is also known as Corchorus olivaceus var. obclavatus, but it seems that Corchorus obclavatus may be a different species altogether, and I couldn't find much information on it. However, I can provide information on Corchorus olivaceus which has similar properties. Corchorus olivaceus is a type of plant that belongs to the mallow family, Malvaceae, and is commonly known as the nalta jute or tossa jute. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestive health. 

The herbal preparations of Corchorus olivaceus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The leaves and stems of the plant are often used in traditional medicine, and the plant is also used as a food source in some parts of the world.
